anchor.snippet: A vector space over a field F is a non-empty set V together with a binary operation and a binary function that satisfy the eight axioms listed below
The binary operation, called vector addition or simply addition assigns to any two vectors
The binary function, called scalar multiplication , assigns to any scalar
When the scalar field is the real numbers , the vector space is called a real vector space

annotations.label: Vector space as module over a field

anchor.snippet: Even more concisely, a vector space is a module over a field
a linear combination of elements of G is an element of V of the form
are said to be linearly independent if no element of G can be written as a linear combination of the other elements of G
A linear subspace or vector subspace W of a vector space V is a non-empty subset of V that is closed under vector addition and scalar multiplication
the linear span or simply the span of G is the smallest linear subspace of V that contains G
A subset of a vector space is a basis if its elements are linearly independent and span the vector space

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis

anchor.snippet: Every vector space has at least one basis, or many in general
all bases of a vector space have the same cardinality , which is called the dimension of the vector space
are called the coordinates of v on the basis
It is thus a vector space isomorphism , which allows translating reasonings and computations
The first example of a vector space consists of arrows in a fixed plane
A second key example of a vector space is provided by pairs of real numbers
The simplest example of a vector space over a field F is the field F itself

anchor.section: Complex numbers and other field extensions
anchor.snippet: The set of complex numbers C , numbers that can be written in the form
Functions from any fixed set Ω to a field F also form vector spaces
Systems of homogeneous linear equations are closely tied to vector spaces
The relation of two vector spaces can be expressed by linear map or linear transformation
An isomorphism is a linear map f : V → W such that there exists an inverse map

annotations.label: Space of linear maps and dual space

anchor.snippet: Linear maps V → W between two vector spaces form a vector space

annotations.label: Classification of vector spaces by dimension

anchor.snippet: two vector spaces over a given field are isomorphic if their dimensions agree and vice versa

annotations.label: Matrix representation of a linear map

anchor.snippet: Matrices are a useful notion to encode linear maps
The determinant det ( A ) of a square matrix A is a scalar that tells whether the associated map is an isomorphism or not
Any nonzero vector v satisfying λ v = f ( v ) , where λ is a scalar, is called an eigenvector of f with eigenvalue λ
the expression on the left hand side can be seen to be a polynomial function in λ , called the characteristic polynomial of f
The set of all eigenvectors corresponding to a particular eigenvalue of f forms a vector space known as the eigenspace

The fundamental Hahn–Banach theorem is concerned with separating subspaces of appropriate topological vector spaces by continuous functionals
Banach spaces , introduced by Stefan Banach , are complete normed vector spaces
and equipped with this norm are called Lebesgue spaces

Imposing boundedness conditions not only on the function, but also on its derivatives leads to Sobolev spaces
Complete inner product spaces are known as Hilbert spaces , in honor of David Hilbert

The spectral theorem decomposes a linear compact operator acting on functions in terms of these eigenfunctions and their eigenvalues
A vector space equipped with an additional bilinear operator defining the multiplication of two vectors is an algebra over a field
forms an algebra known as the polynomial ring
Another crucial example are Lie algebras , which are neither commutative nor associative

A vector bundle is a family of vector spaces parametrized continuously by a topological space X
The case dim V = 1 is called a line bundle
the tangent bundle consists of the collection of tangent spaces parametrized by the points of a differentiable manifold
by the hairy ball theorem , there is no (tangent) vector field on the 2-sphere
The cotangent bundle of a differentiable manifold consists, at every point of the manifold, of the dual of the tangent space
Modules are to rings what vector spaces are to fields
those modules that do (including all vector spaces) are known as free modules
an affine space is a set with a free transitive vector space action
an affine subspace is a subset of W obtained by translating a linear subspace V by a fixed vector
The set of one-dimensional subspaces of a fixed finite-dimensional vector space V is known as projective space
